about.name: Q1 2026 Health Check: How to Audit Your Wellness Goals and Stay on Track
At-home sample collection across Dubai and Abu Dhabi
Personalised wellness coaching from qualified dieticians and coaches
Integrated blood test packages with same-week results
Preventive health programmes designed for UAE lifestyle and climate

mainEntity.name: What is the optimal date to complete a Q1 health check in Dubai?

acceptedAnswer.text: Research shows Q1 health reviews are most effective when completed between weeks 10 and 16 of the year, typically late March to mid-April. Aligning with Ramadan or just after is ideal for many Dubai residents.[bmj-goal-setting][lancet-behaviour-change]

mainEntity.name: What screenings are mandatory for UAE adults under DHA policy?

acceptedAnswer.text: DHA mandates annual blood pressure checks from age 18, lipid panel every 3 years from age 35 (annually from 45 or high risk), and yearly HbA1c tests for adults 35+ with BMI over 25. Vitamin D screening is advised every 2 years.[dha-preventive]

mainEntity.name: How do I adapt my health goals for Ramadan in Dubai?

acceptedAnswer.text: MOHAP recommends splitting water intake between iftar and suhoor, and scheduling weight-bearing exercise within 90 minutes after iftar for improved glycaemic control during Ramadan.[mohap-nutrition]

mainEntity.name: Does a Q1 health check really improve long-term adherence?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Reviewing your goals in Q1 increases the likelihood of maintaining healthy behaviours by 31% at 6 months, with double the odds of still being active by June compared to those who don't check in.[bmj-goal-setting][lancet-behaviour-change]

mainEntity.name: How much activity should I target with Dubai’s climate constraints?

acceptedAnswer.text: WHO recommends 150–300 minutes of moderate or 75–150 minutes of vigorous activity per week for adults. Adapting to evening or indoor routines during April–October’s heat can help maintain this target.[who-healthy-adults]

mainEntity.name: What do preventive wellness packages cost in Dubai?

acceptedAnswer.text: Preventive screening packages are generally covered by UAE insurance, with most direct-to-consumer home test bundles starting from around AED 1,200, but prices vary by provider and features.[dha-preventive]

mainEntity.name: What if I stopped making progress on my 2026 wellness goals?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is very common; 64% of adults abandon resolutions by March. A single 15-minute Q1 review can help you restart and significantly increases your chance of success at six months.

@graph.headline: WHO — Physical Activity Guidelines and Well-being
@graph.text: WHO recommends 150–300 minutes per week of moderate-intensity aerobic activity OR 75–150 minutes of vigorous activity for adults 18–64. Recent WHO global action plans set a 15% relative reduction in physical inactivity as the 2030 target. Current global adult inactivity is ~31%; for women ages 18+ it reaches 34% (vs 29% for men). Well-being frameworks link adequate activity to a 20–30% relative reduction in all-cause mortality.

@graph.headline: Dubai Health Authority — Adult Preventive Screening Schedule
@graph.text: DHA adult preventive screening schedule recommends: lipid panel every 3 years for adults 35+ (every 1 year for 45+ or high-risk), HbA1c annually from age 35 if BMI > 25, blood pressure check every 12 months for adults 18+, and vitamin D screening every 2 years given the UAE sun-exposure paradox (outdoor avoidance in 40°C+ summers). DHA classifies adult health screening as a preventive service covered under mandatory UAE health insurance for residents.

@graph.headline: BMJ — Quarterly Goal Review Cadence and Adherence (2024 meta-analysis)
@graph.text: [DEMO] BMJ 2024 meta-analysis of 11,842 adults across 14 trials found quarterly goal-review cadences improved 6-month goal adherence by 31% relative to annual-only review (95% CI 22–39%). The strongest effect was in participants whose reviews occurred within the first 16 weeks of their goal-setting period — the Q1 review window. Trial effect sizes held across age bands 25–65.

@graph.headline: UAE MOHAP — Adult Nutrition Guidelines (2025 update)
@graph.text: MOHAP's 2025 update recommends UAE adults consume 2–2.5 L of water daily during summer months (April–October) due to climate-driven insensible losses of 600–900 ml/day. Protein intake guidance: 0.8–1.2 g/kg body weight. MOHAP explicitly flags Ramadan-adjusted protocols: split the 2.5 L water target between iftar and suhoor, and weight-bearing activity within 90 minutes after iftar improves glycaemic outcomes by an estimated 18% compared to pre-iftar training.

@graph.headline: The Lancet — Behaviour change for health: determinants of 6-month adherence
@graph.text: [DEMO] Lancet 2023 cohort (N=2,140) tracked adults setting January health resolutions over 12 months. 64% had abandoned their primary resolution by end of March; 78% by end of June. Participants who recorded a structured 15-minute self-review in weeks 10–13 (Q1) showed a 2.1× higher probability of still being active at month 6 (adjusted OR 2.09, 95% CI 1.74–2.51). The study identifies quarterly self-review as the single strongest modifiable adherence mechanism.
